drift.title: {{name}} has drifted
drift.hint: since the manifest. Save changes to update the profile, or Repair to restore it.
drift.outOfSyncFallback: profile and disk are out of sync

following.title: No profiles yet
following.hint: Create a profile to save your mod configuration, or import a code from a friend.

published.title: You haven't published anything yet
published.hint: Share a profile to make it appear here. Friends paste your code to install the same set of mods.

library.storageHelp: Mod Library still shows every installed mod. Active means the files are in the game folder; inactive means they are kept installed but moved out of the active game folder. Profile checkboxes decide which profiles reference the mod.
library.storeAria: Disable {{mod}} in game and keep it installed
library.readOnlyTitle: Followed profiles are read-only. Duplicate the profile to edit it.

empty.title: No installed mods
empty.hint: Install mods first, then use Mod Library to choose which profiles include each one.

noMatches.title: No matching mods
noMatches.hint: Try a different name, folder, ID, or version.

library.toastStored: {{mod}} disabled in game and kept installed
library.toastBulkStored_one: {{count}} unused active mod disabled in game
library.toastBulkStored_other: {{count}} unused active mods disabled in game
library.toastBulkStorageFailed: Disabled {{stored}} of {{total}} unused active mods. Failed: {{mods}}

loadOrder.title: Load order for {{name}}
loadOrder.subtitle: Top loads first. STS2 can still re-sort dependencies if an order is invalid.
loadOrder.note: Drag rows or use the arrow buttons. This order is stored with the profile and written to STS2 settings when the profile is active or activated.
loadOrder.empty: This profile has no mods to order.
loadOrder.toastSavedApplied: Load order saved for {{name}} and applied to settings.save.
loadOrder.toastSavedInactive: Load order saved for {{name}}. It will apply when you activate this profile.
loadOrder.toastSavedSettingsMissing: Load order saved for {{name}}, but settings.save was not found. Launch STS2 once, then activate this profile.
loadOrder.toastSavedSettingsMultiple: Load order saved for {{name}}, but multiple settings.save files were found so none were changed.
loadOrder.toastSavedGameRunning: Load order saved for {{name}}, but STS2 is running. Close the game and activate the profile to apply it.
loadOrder.toastSavedSettingsFailed: Load order saved for {{name}}, but settings.save could not be updated.

card.restore: Re-apply this profile to match the manifest
card.shareTitle: Share — friends paste the code to install
card.reShareTitle: Re-share — same code, friends see an update

kebab.copyShareMessage: Copy share message (paste-ready: intro + link + code)
kebab.copyInstallLinkTitle: Copy install link (clickable in Discord / chat)

switch.title: Switch away from "{{name}}"?
switch.body: This profile has unsaved changes on disk. Switching applies another manifest and those working changes will not be saved to the current profile.
switch.warning: Use Save changes first if you want the current install to become the profile manifest.

delete.title: Delete profile "{{name}}"?
delete.body: The profile manifest will be removed. Mod files on disk stay where they are.

repair.title: Repair "{{name}}"?
repair.bodyWithOrphans: Re-applies the manifest and moves {{count}} active mod(s) that aren't in the profile to mods_disabled: {{list}}.
repair.bodyNoOrphans: Re-applies the manifest. No active extra mods to disable — drift will be fixed by toggling state.

toast.savedChangesWithReShare: Saved changes to "{{name}}". Re-share when you're ready to publish them.
toast.importedModpack: Imported modpack "{{name}}" — {{count}} mods. You're now subscribed for updates!
toast.installLinkCopied: Install link copied — paste into Discord / chat
toast.shareMessageCopied: Share message copied — paste into Discord / chat

search.noResults: No mods found. Try different keywords.

browseModpacks.title: Browse Modpacks
browseModpacks.cached: Showing cached results — couldn't reach GitHub.
browseModpacks.rateLimited: GitHub is rate-limiting us — try again in a minute, or connect a GitHub token in Settings for a higher limit.
browseModpacks.noPacks: No public modpacks found yet — be the first to share one!
browseModpacks.cta: Want yours here too? Publish a new pack — or re-share an existing one — from Profiles and pick <1>Public</1> visibility.
browseModpacks.largePackNote: Large packs can take a while while bundles download from the curator's GitHub repo. Don't close the window.
browseModpacks.betaTitle: The public modpack browser is still being tuned.

app.vanillaDesc: Launches Slay the Spire 2 with all mods temporarily disabled (auto-backup first).
app.gameRunningDesc: Mod and profile changes are paused until the game closes — touching files now can crash the game.
app.willRestart: The app will restart when this finishes.
app.downloadManual: Open GitHub releases page to download manually
app.nexusPendingToast: Opened {{modName}} on Nexus. Click "Slow Download" / "Manual" — the app will auto-install when the zip lands in your Downloads folder.
app.updateConfirmBody: This will download and re-install the latest GitHub release for each. Frozen mods are skipped. Make sure STS2 is closed first.
app.updateFailed: Update failed: {{error}}. If you installed via .deb or .rpm, use the AppImage build for seamless updates, or click Download to get the new package manually.

toast.launchingVanilla: Launching STS2 in vanilla mode (all mods disabled, backup created)...

toast.preservedConfigs_other: Preserved {{count}} config files you edited in "{{mod}}": {{files}}

tutorial.title: Tutorial
tutorial.subtitle: Bookmark this page or send the link to a friend.

player.tipLaunch: <0>Ctrl+L</0> launches the active modpack from anywhere in the app.
player.fullReferenceIntro: For when something doesn't fit the quick start above. Most players never need any of this.

quickStart.title: Play your friend's modpack in under a minute

step1.title: Click the link, or paste the code
step1.body: Your friend sends you a code like <0>jess/AA5A-315D-61AE</0> or a clickable install link. <1>Click the link</1> — it opens a small page that pops up this app with a confirm dialog showing exactly what's about to install. <3>Or paste the code</3> into the <5>Drop a code, hit Add</5> box on <7>Home</7>. Same result either way: GitHub-sourced and bundled mods download automatically; any Nexus-only mods show as pending so you know what to grab manually.

step2.title: Hit Launch
step2.body: Top-right of the window, or press <0>Ctrl/⌘ L</0>. The game opens through Steam with your friend's exact mods enabled. The app auto-backs-up before every launch so it's always reversible.

step3.title: Stay in sync
step3.body: When your friend updates their pack you'll see an <0>Update available</0> card on Home. One click pulls the diff and re-applies their pack (Nexus changes still need a manual download from Nexus). Your save is unaffected.

cheatItems.switchActiveDesc: Top-bar profile chip → pick a pack.
cheatItems.updateEverythingDesc: Profiles → Update all on the active pack.
cheatItems.pinModDesc: Mods → kebab on a mod → Freeze this mod.
cheatItems.rollBackDesc: Settings → Backups → Restore. Auto-saved before every launch.
cheatItems.auditModDesc: Settings → Audit → Run audit. See current vs latest at a glance.
cheatItems.launchVanillaDesc: Top-bar Vanilla button — ignores your active profile.
cheatItems.addSingleModDesc: Quick-add a GitHub or Nexus URL on Home.
cheatItems.shareYourPackDesc: Home hero → Copy link (or Copy code / Copy message). Drop in Discord.
cheatItems.openFriendLinkDesc: Click their install link in chat — the manager pops up with a confirm.

step1.title: First-time setup
step1.p1: On first launch the app tries to auto-detect Slay the Spire 2 from your Steam install. If the status dot in the bottom-left says <0>Game detected</0>, you're done with this step.
step1.p2_gamePath: → <0>Game Path</0> and either click <2>Browse...</2> or paste the folder where the game files live. The signature file the app checks for depends on your OS: <4>SlayTheSpire2.exe</4> on Windows, <6>SlayTheSpire2.app</6> on macOS, <8>SlayTheSpire2.pck</8> on Linux. The default location on Windows is <10>C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Slay the Spire 2</10>.

step2.title: Optional: API keys
step2.p1: You don't need either of these to play with a friend's modpack — they only help with mods you find yourself.
step2.nexusDesc: sign up free at nexusmods.com → Profile → API Keys → "Personal API key". Paste into Settings → Nexus Mods API Key. Lets you Quick-Add Nexus links and browse Nexus mods inside the app.
step2.nexusNote: Free-tier only. The app catches Nexus zips that land in your Downloads folder when you use Slow / Manual downloads. Nexus Premium's instant-download API isn't wired in, so paid subscribers don't get faster downloads here.
step2.githubDesc: only needed if you plan to share modpacks (see the Modpack Creator tab above). Otherwise skip.

step3.title: Sharing & receiving packs — code vs. link
step3.p1: Every share surface in the app gives you three interchangeable ways to pass a pack to a friend, paired side-by-side: <0>Copy code</0>, <1>Copy link</1>, and <2>Copy message</2>. You'll find them on the Home hero, in each "Your other packs" row, on every Profiles row (inline next to the code), and in the kebab menu.
step3.codeDesc: like <0>jess/AA5A-315D-61AE</0>. The raw thing. Friend pastes it into the manager's "Drop a code, hit Add" box on Home.
step3.linkDesc: like <0>https://mohamedserhan.github.io/sts2-mod-manager/i.html?c=jess/AA5A-315D-61AE</0>. Discord / Slack / iMessage all turn this into a clickable URL because it's plain HTTPS. Friend clicks → lands on a tiny install-bridge page → clicks "Open in STS2 Mod Manager" → the manager pops up with the confirm dialog. Friends who don't have the manager yet get download links on the same page.
step3.messageDesc: a paste-ready one-block message containing both the link and the raw code, plus an intro line ("Join my Slay the Spire 2 modpack…"). Drop it in a chat and you're done.
step3.whyNotSts2mm: <0>Why not just paste <1>sts2mm://</1> URLs directly?</0> Discord and most chat apps only auto-linkify <2>http://</2> and <3>https://</3> — custom protocol schemes show as un-clickable text. The HTTPS bridge page exists exactly to make the link clickable everywhere.
step3.smartRouting: <0>Smart routing on the receiving end.</0> When a friend pastes a code or clicks a link, the manager checks what state they're in and shows the right dialog: brand-new pack → confirm + install; they already have it but it's not active → "Switch to it?"; an update is pending → "Apply update?"; already on the latest → friendly "you're up to date" toast. No guessing which case applies.

step4.title: Adding individual mods
step4.p1: On Home there's a Quick Add box at the top. Paste any of these:
step4.githubItem: A GitHub repo URL (e.g. <0>https://github.com/owner/repo</0>) — the app installs the latest release automatically.
step4.nexusItem: A Nexus Mods URL (e.g. <0>https://www.nexusmods.com/slaythespire2/mods/123</0>) — the app opens the mod's <2>Files</2> tab in your browser. Click Nexus's <4>Slow Download</4> (sometimes labelled <6>Manual</6>), wait the few seconds, and let your browser save the zip to <8>~/Downloads</8>. The app's downloads-folder watcher picks it up and installs it for you. <10>Don't click <12>Mod Manager Download</12></10> — that uses Nexus's <14>nxm://</14> deep link, which isn't wired through to the install pipeline yet, so nothing happens when you click it.
step4.dragZip: You can also drag a <0>.zip</0> file straight into the app window to install it.

step5.title: Turning mods on/off
step5.p1: Mods tab. Toggle individual mods on/off. <0>Disabling a mod doesn't delete it</0> — its files are moved to <2>mods_disabled/</2> alongside <4>mods/</4> in your game folder. Turning a mod back on is instant, no re-download. To permanently remove a mod, use the kebab menu → <6>Remove mod…</6>.

step6.title: Vanilla play
step6.p1: Top-bar <0>Vanilla</0> button. Starts the game with all mods temporarily disabled. The app creates an auto-backup first so the next launch puts everything back exactly as it was.

step7.title: Backups & recovery
step7.p1: The app creates an auto-backup before every game launch and before Vanilla Mode. Settings → Backups lists them with timestamp + size. <0>Restore</0> rolls your mods folder back to that snapshot. Only the newest 5 are kept.

step8.title: Reporting bugs
step8.p1: Settings → scroll to <0>In-app logs</0>. The viewer has filter chips (Info / Warn / Error / Debug) and a <2>Send to support</2> button that opens a prefilled GitHub issue with the recent log tail. The log captures every download, every profile apply, every error.

creator.intro: For modpack creators. A modpack is a named profile that captures every mod in your install (with its version + source). Friends paste your share code; their app downloads the same mods from the same sources. You don't host any files yourself — share data lives in a public repo (<0>sts2mm-profiles</0>) on your GitHub account that the app creates and updates for you.

step1.title: Set up a GitHub token
step1.p1: Sharing requires a GitHub token with permission to create + update a repo on your account.
step1.li1: Go to GitHub → Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Fine-grained tokens (or "Tokens (classic)" — both work).
step1.li2: Give it <0>repo</0> access (classic) or <1>Contents: Read and write</1> + <2>Administration: Read and write</2> (fine-grained — Administration is only needed for the one-time repo create, you can drop it after).
step1.li3_suffix: → <0>GitHub Token</0> and hit Save. The token is stored in your OS keyring, not in plain text on disk.

step2.title: Install the mods you want in your modpack
step2.p1: Use Quick Add for GitHub/Nexus links, drag-and-drop a <0>.zip</0>, or use the Browse tab. For mods that don't auto-link to a source: open Mods view → expand the mod → click <2>Auto-detect source</2> or paste the GitHub repo manually. Mods without a known source can still be shared (the app bundles a copy in your sharing repo) but linking the source is preferred — your friends get the canonical release.

step3.title: Create a profile from your current state
step3.p1: Open <0>Profiles</0> → <1>Create Profile</1>. Pick a name (e.g. "Co-op night" or "Daily-cheese-build"). The app captures your current mods folder — everything currently installed, with each mod's name, folder, version, and source.

step4.title: Share it — three interchangeable formats
step4.p1: On the Profiles row for your new profile, click the <0>Share</0> (paper plane) icon. The app uploads the manifest to your <1>sts2mm-profiles</1> repo (auto-creating the repo on first use, public, on your GitHub account) and gives you back a share code plus a success modal with three copy buttons. The same trio is available on every share surface (Home hero chip, Profiles inline chip, kebab menu, Other Packs row):
step4.codeItem: <0>Copy code</0> — the raw <1>you/AA5A-315D-61AE</1>. Friends paste it into the <3>Drop a code, hit Add</3> box on Home. Best for tight spaces (Twitter, terse DMs).
step4.linkItem: <0>Copy link</0> — an HTTPS install URL pointing at the manager's bridge page on GitHub Pages. Discord, Slack, iMessage, Reddit etc. <2>all</2> auto-linkify this because it's plain HTTPS. Friend clicks → bridge page opens → bridge page fires the install handler in the manager → confirm dialog. Friends without the manager get download links on the same page.
step4.messageItem: <0>Copy message</0> — a paste-ready one-block message containing both the link and the raw code, plus an intro line. Drop it in chat and you're done.
step4.whyNotSts2mm: <0>Why we don't just hand out <1>sts2mm://</1> URLs directly:</0> Discord and most chat apps only auto-linkify <2>http://</2> and <3>https://</3>. A raw <4>sts2mm://import/...</4> shows as un-clickable plain text the recipient has to select-copy and paste into a browser address bar. The HTTPS bridge page exists exactly to make the share link clickable everywhere a friend might paste it.
step4.smartRouting: The smart router on the receiving side handles brand-new installs, re-activations of an already-installed pack, pending updates, and "you're already on this" no-ops — your friend doesn't have to think about which case applies.

step5.title: Updating your modpack
step5.p1: Change your installed mods (install, upgrade, remove). Then on the same Profiles row, click the <0>Re-share</0> (refresh) icon. <2>Same code, updated content.</2> Your friends' apps detect the change next time they open the app and show an Update card on Home.
step5.p2: If you want a separate version (e.g. v2 of your modpack with breaking changes), <0>Duplicate</0> the profile, rename, and Share that one — it gets its own code.

step6.title: What gets shared (and what doesn't)
step6.shared: <0>Shared:</0> the profile name, the list of mods, each mod's name + folder name + mod_id + version, and a source per mod (a GitHub repo, or — for mods without a public source — a direct download URL pointing back to the bundled copy in your repo).
step6.notShared: <0>Not shared:</0> your local mod files themselves (unless they need to be bundled), your save data, your API keys, anything outside the profile manifest.
step6.visibility: <0>Visibility:</0> the <1>sts2mm-profiles</1> repo is public — anyone with your share code can read it, and the repo itself is browsable on github.com under your account. Don't put anything secret in a profile name or in the bundled mod files.

step7.title: Curator best practices
step7.audit: <0>Audit before sharing.</0> Settings → Audit → <1>Run audit</1> flags mods without a known source — link them first, otherwise they get bundled into your sharing repo (bigger footprint, slower for friends to download).
step7.pin: <0>Freeze versions.</0> Mods → expand a mod → <1>Freeze</1> — freezing prevents auto-updates and profile changes so your modpack stays reproducible.
step7.test: <0>Test as a friend.</0> The fastest way to validate a share is to ask a friend to paste the code on a clean install and confirm they see the same mods enabled.
step7.sendLog: <0>Send your log if things break.</0> Settings → In-app logs. The viewer's <1>Send to support</1> button opens a GitHub issue prefilled with the recent log tail — detailed enough to diagnose most "it didn't work for my friend" reports.

autoDetect.title: Auto-detect sources
autoDetect.subtitle: Scan installed mods against GitHub by name. Review matches before applying — high-confidence matches are pre-selected.
autoDetect.allLinkedTitle: Nothing to detect — every mod already has a source.
autoDetect.allLinkedBody: All {{count}} installed mod(s) have a GitHub or Nexus link attached, so auto-detect left them alone. To re-link a specific mod manually, open the Mods view, expand the row, and edit its source.
autoDetect.allLinkedBody_one: All {{count}} installed mod has a GitHub or Nexus link attached, so auto-detect left it alone. To re-link a specific mod manually, open the Mods view, expand the row, and edit its source.
autoDetect.allLinkedBody_other: All {{count}} installed mods have a GitHub or Nexus link attached, so auto-detect left them alone. To re-link a specific mod manually, open the Mods view, expand the row, and edit its source.

browseDetail.whatHappensBody: We fetch the latest GitHub release for {{repo}}, download the first asset, and place it in your mods folder. Updates check this repo whenever you run an audit.
browseDetail.nexusInstallBody: Nexus doesn't allow direct downloads — clicking install opens the mod's Files tab in your browser. Click Slow Download (or Manual) — not Mod Manager Download — wait the few seconds, and the manager auto-installs the .zip as soon as it lands in your Downloads folder.

diagnosticBundle.title: Generate support bundle
diagnosticBundle.subtitle: Builds a single text report — game info, active profile, mod list, and recent logs. Nothing is uploaded; the bundle is copied to your clipboard so you can paste it wherever.
diagnosticBundle.readyToast: Bundle ready — scroll the preview to copy manually.
diagnosticBundle.reportGitHubIssueTitle: Bug report from STS2 Mod Manager

logsViewer.emptyLog: Log is empty — actions in the app will appear here.

sourceEditor.title: Sources for {{name}}
sourceEditor.subtitle: Linking enables auto-updates from GitHub releases or Nexus.
sourceEditor.displayNameHint: Optional label shown in the manager only. The manifest name stays unchanged.
sourceEditor.displayDescriptionHint: Optional manager-only description for confusing or untranslated mods.
sourceEditor.notePlaceholder: e.g. downloaded from Patreon, compat patch for v1.8 build
sourceEditor.noteHint: Free-form. Shown on the mod row so you remember where this came from.
sourceEditor.otherLinkHint: For mods that don't live on GitHub or Nexus.
sourceEditor.tagsHint: Optional manager-only categories. Separate tags with commas.
sourceEditor.nexusOnlyMessage: Nexus-only mod — try fetching the GitHub repo from the Nexus description so updates are checked from both sources.

step1.title: Find your Slay the Spire 2 install
step1.subtitle: We'll auto-detect via Steam. You can override anytime in Settings.
step1.pathError: This folder doesn't look like a Slay the Spire 2 install (no {{signature}} found) — pick the install root.

step2.title: Connect your accounts (optional)
step2.subtitle: Nexus needs an API key for downloads. GitHub auth raises the rate limit. Skip both to start with public Browse only.
step2.nexusSaved: Saved — Nexus mods will appear in Browse.
step2.nexusSkipHint: Skip if you only use GitHub mods.
step2.nexusFreeNote: Free Nexus account is fine. To install a Nexus mod, you'll click "Slow Download" / "Manual" on Nexus — the app catches the zip from your Downloads folder. (Nexus Premium's instant-download API isn't wired in.)
step2.ghSaved: Saved — Browse will use authenticated calls.
step2.ghSkipHint: Skipping is fine — you'll just hit rate limits faster on Browse.
step2.ghRequiredNote: <0>Required to publish modpacks</0> — needs <1>repo</1> scope (classic PAT) or <2>Contents: R/W</2> + <3>Administration: R/W</3> (fine-grained).

step3.title: Pick your first profile
step3.subtitle: Start vanilla, follow a friend's pack, or import a JSON. You can always switch.

optionVanilla.title: Vanilla — no mods
optionVanilla.desc: Game launches stock — toggle mods on later

optionFollow.title: Follow a friend (paste code)
optionFollow.desc: Type or paste a code like jess/XYZ4

optionImport.title: Import profile JSON
optionImport.desc: Drop or pick a .json exported from another install

optionSkip.title: Skip — set up later
optionSkip.desc: You can re-run setup from Settings → General

publish.publishSubtitle: Uploads your mod files to a GitHub repo on your account so friends can install them.
publish.reshareSubtitle: Same code — followers will see an update prompt next time they open the app.
publish.sharedSubtitle: Anyone with the code can install this exact set of mods.
publish.tokenExplanation: Publishing uploads your mod files to a GitHub repo on your account, so the manager needs a personal access token with <0>repo</0> scope (or, for fine-grained tokens, <1>Contents: R/W</1> + <2>Administration: R/W</2>).
publish.friendsOnlyDesc: Anyone with the code or link can install it. Not listed on Browse Modpacks.
publish.publicDesc: Listed on Browse Modpacks. Anyone using the app can find and install it.
publish.publicRepoNote: The manager will create a <0>public</0> repo called <1>sts2mm-profiles</1> on your GitHub account (if you don't already have one) to hold your published packs + mod bundles. You can re-publish updates later to the same code; you can also delete or make it private on GitHub at any time.
publish.progressNote: This can take a minute or two for big packs — your mod files are being zipped + uploaded to your <0>sts2mm-profiles</0> repo one at a time. Don't close the window.
publish.copyCodeTitle: Copy the raw share code (paste into the manager's code box)
publish.copyLinkTitle: Copy the install link — clickable in Discord / chat, routes friends through the install bridge page
publish.copyMessageTitle: Copy a paste-ready share message — intro line + install link + raw code in one block
publish.reuseNote: This same code is reused if you re-share later — friends will see updates instead of having to follow a new code.
publish.openRepo: Open my profiles repo on GitHub
publish.failedUploads_one: {{count}} mod failed to upload: {{list}}. Friends installing this code will see "missing mod" for these — re-publish to retry.
publish.failedUploads_other: {{count}} mods failed to upload: {{list}}{{more}}. Friends installing this code will see "missing mod" for these — re-publish to retry.
publish.failedUploadsToast_one: {{count}} mod failed to upload: {{list}}. Friends installing this code will see "missing mod" for these — try re-publishing.
publish.failedUploadsToast_other: {{count}} mods failed to upload: {{list}}{{more}}. Friends installing this code will see "missing mod" for these — try re-publishing.
publish.failedUploadsHelp: Friends installing this code will see "missing mod" for these - re-publish to retry.

shareImport.shareMessageJoinPack: Join my Slay the Spire 2 modpack "{{packName}}":
shareImport.shareMessagePasteCode: Or paste this code in the manager: {{code}}
shareImport.installConfirmWarning: Modpacks run code (DLLs) inside Slay the Spire 2. Only install from creators you trust.
shareImport.updateDialogCuratorPushed_one: The curator has pushed <1>{{count}}</1> change since you last synced:
shareImport.updateDialogCuratorPushed_other: The curator has pushed <1>{{count}}</1> changes since you last synced:
shareImport.switchDialogActivatePrompt: Activate it now to switch your active modpack? You can switch back to your current saved manifest any time.

home.unlinkBody: You'll stop receiving updates from the curator. The mods stay installed.
home.repairPackBody: Repair wipes the mods folder and reinstalls every entry from the manifest. Use when the install has drifted or you suspect corruption.
home.switchAwayBody: This profile has unsaved changes on disk. Switching applies another manifest and those working changes will not be saved to the current profile.
home.switchAwayWarning: Open Profiles and use Save changes first if you want to keep them.
home.noLaunchTitle: Use the Launch button in the top bar
home.manageModsTitle: See and toggle the mods in this profile
home.shareThisPackTitle: Publish this pack to a GitHub repo on your account so friends can install it with a code or link
home.clickToCopyShareCode: Click to copy the share code
home.repairOrphanBody: Re-applies the manifest and deletes {{count}} mod file(s) that aren't in the profile: {{list}}.
home.repairOrphanBodyNoOrphans: Re-applies the manifest exactly. Toggles, versions, and load order are restored from the saved snapshot.
home.repairOrphanWarning: Orphan files will be permanently removed. Restore from a backup if you change your mind.
home.importingOverlayTitle: Downloading mods and setting up your profile…
home.importingOverlaySubtext: This may take a few minutes for large modpacks.
home.gameNotDetectedDesc: Slay the Spire 2 wasn't found automatically. Open Settings to set the path.
home.reapplyTitle: Re-apply the manifest and delete any orphan mod files
home.shortcutTip: Tip: press {{shortcut}} from anywhere to launch this pack.
home.viewAllInProfilesTitle: Open the Profiles page to manage every pack
home.copyInstallLinkTitle: Copy install link — clickable in Discord / chat, routes friends through the install bridge
home.copyFullMessageTitle: Copy full share message — intro line + install link + raw code
home.pasteCodeAbove: Paste their share code in the box above.

toast.allUpToDate: All modpacks are up to date!
toast.installedPartial: Installed {{installed}}/{{total}} mods. Missing: {{missing}}. These need to be installed manually.
toast.profileNotFoundOnDisk: Couldn't find this profile on disk.
toast.syncedModpack: Synced modpack "{{name}}" - you're up to date!

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure game paths, accounts, and preferences

general.couldNotVerify: Couldn't verify {{file}} in this folder. Pick the install root, not a subfolder.
general.autoDetectHint: Click Auto-detect to find your Steam install, or Browse to pick the folder manually.
general.couldNotAutoDetect: Could not auto-detect game path. Please set it manually.
general.launchDesc: How the Launch button and Ctrl/⌘ L start Slay the Spire 2.
general.steamDesc: Launches via Steam. Required for cloud saves, achievements, and Proton on Linux.
general.directDesc: Skips the Steam launcher and runs the game executable. Steam itself still needs to be running — STS2 uses Steamworks for saves and achievements. Useful for Family Sharing borrowers (the lender's library lock blocks normal Steam launches) and Steam offline mode. Not supported for Proton/Linux installs.

accounts.nexusSavedHelp: Saved · Nexus mods will appear in Browse.
accounts.nexusApiLink: Get your API key from Nexus Mods
accounts.nexusTierNote: Free-tier only. To install a Nexus mod, click the "Slow Download" / "Manual" button on Nexus — the app catches the zip from your Downloads folder. The "Mod Manager Download" button isn't wired through. Nexus Premium's instant-download API isn't either, so paid subscribers don't get faster downloads here.
accounts.githubSavedHelp: Saved · raises API rate limit to 5,000 req/hr.
accounts.githubHelp: Optional for browsing GitHub mods (raises rate limit to 5,000 req/hr from 60/hr without auth). Required if you want to publish modpacks.
accounts.githubClassicPat: Classic PAT — check the repo scope.
accounts.githubFinePat: Fine-grained PAT — repository access scoped to sts2mm-profiles (or "All repositories"), with Contents: Read and write + Administration: Read and write (Administration is only needed for the one-time repo create; you can drop it after).

backups.title: Backups
backups.desc: Auto-saved before every launch and Vanilla Mode - keeps the last 5
backups.noBackupsHint: A backup is auto-created before every launch. You can also create one manually.
backups.restoreConfirmBody: This will replace your current mods with the snapshot. Your current state is not saved unless you back it up first.
backups.restoreCheckbox: Save current as a new backup before restoring
backups.deleteConfirmBody: You won't be able to restore it after this.

audit.title: Mod audit
audit.desc: Compare each installed mod against its source · freeze to keep the current version and on/off state
audit.noAuditHint: Run an audit to see which mods have updates available, which are frozen, and which can't be matched against a source.
audit.goneTitle: GitHub releases exist but have no installable assets — auto-update can't fetch from this source
audit.snoozedTitle: This update is skipped until a newer version appears.
audit.skipUpdateTooltip: Hide v{{version}} until a newer version appears.
audit.showUpdateAgainTooltip: Clear the skip and show this update again.
audit.snoozedToast: Skipped updates for '{{name}}' until the next release.
audit.updateBlockedTitle: The newest release requires a different STS2 game version.
audit.pinTooltip: Freeze — keep this exact version and on/off state. Updates and modpack/profile changes will skip it.
audit.unpinTooltip: Unfreeze — allow updates and profile changes again

settings.onLatestRelease: On the source's latest installable release.
settings.updateGameBlocked: Latest {{version}} requires game {{required}}. Will install the newest compatible release: {{compatibleVersion}}.
settings.unpinnedToast: Unfrozen '{{name}}' — updates will be checked again.
settings.pinnedToast: Frozen '{{name}}' — updates and profile changes will skip it.
settings.noAssetTooltip: GitHub release {{version}} ships no installable asset. Auto-update can't fetch from this source.
settings.auditGameVersionBlock: This mod's manifest declares min_game_version={{declared}}. Your STS2 install reports {{installed}}. Until you update STS2 (or switch beta branches), the game's loader will silently skip this mod.
settings.auditWontLoad: Won't load on your game (needs Slay the Spire 2 ≥ v{{version}}; you have v{{installed}}). Use Repair on the Mods row to roll back to a compatible release.
settings.auditCompatTag: Latest release {{latest}} requires game {{required}}. Update will walk back to {{compatible}}, the newest release compatible with your STS2 build.
settings.auditCompatTagVisible: Latest {{latest}} needs game {{required}}; Update will install {{compatible}} (newest compatible).
settings.auditAlreadyCompat: Latest release {{version}} requires game {{required}}. You're already on {{installed}}, the newest release that runs on your current STS2 build. Update STS2 (or switch beta branches) to pick up the newer mod release.
settings.auditAlreadyCompatVisible: Latest {{version}} needs game {{required}}; you're on {{installed}} - the newest version that runs on your game.

mods.repairConfirmBody: This will delete the current on-disk install and re-download the latest release from the linked GitHub source. Use it when a mod is broken (version reads "unknown", game won't load it, etc.). Make sure STS2 is closed.
mods.deleteConfirmBody: This permanently removes the mod files from disk. This cannot be undone.
mods.deleteAllConfirmBody: Every mod folder will be permanently removed from disk. This cannot be undone.
mods.deleteAllConfirmWarning: Profiles that include these mods will be unable to launch until mods are reinstalled.
mods.advancedTitle: Show source pills, Freeze/Delete, source editor
mods.pinnedTitle: Frozen — updates and modpack/profile changes will skip this mod's version and enabled/disabled state
mods.latestTitle: On the source's latest installable release.
mods.updateClickTitle: Click to update from v{{current}} → v{{target}}. Same pipeline as Settings → Audit.
mods.pinDesc: Don't update, replace, enable, or disable this mod until you unfreeze it. Use when beta updates or modpacks would break your setup.
mods.unpinDesc: Allow updates, replacements, and profile/modpack changes again.
mods.repairDesc: Force-reinstall from GitHub. Use when version reads "unknown", the game refuses to load this mod, or the install otherwise looks broken.
mods.repairNeedSource: Link a GitHub source first via "Edit sources…" — repair fetches from GitHub.
mods.subtitle: {{total}} installed · {{enabled}} active{{disabled}}
mods.openModLibraryTitle: Choose which profiles include each installed mod without enabling or disabling it.
mods.checking: Checking each mod against its source…
mods.checkForUpdates: Check each mod against its source for updates.
mods.allUpToDate: Every linked mod is on its source's latest installable release.
mods.updateAllTitle: Update every GitHub-linked mod with a pending update. Frozen mods are skipped.
mods.quickAddLabel: GitHub URL, Nexus URL, or github:owner/repo
mods.emptyDescription: Browse to install a mod, paste a friend's profile code, or drop a .zip anywhere on this window.
mods.disambiguatorTitle: Two installed mods are named "{{name}}". This one is identified by {{identifier}}.
mods.nexusUpdateTitle: Nexus has v{{nexusVer}} (you have v{{localVer}}). Click to open the Nexus Files tab — pick "Slow Download" / "Manual" and the manager will auto-install the zip when it lands in Downloads.
mods.snoozedTitle: Update suggestion snoozed at v{{version}}. Auto-expires when a newer release appears.
mods.gameVersionBlockedTitle: Newer release exists (v{{target}}) but it requires a newer Slay the Spire 2 build. Update STS2 (or switch beta branches) to pick it up; the manager can install an older compatible release in the meantime.
mods.minGameVersionTitle: This mod's manifest declares min_game_version={{minVer}}. Your STS2 is v{{yourVer}}. The game's loader will silently skip this mod until you update STS2 or switch beta branches. Use Repair (advanced kebab) to roll back to a compatible release.
mods.unsnoozeDesc: Show the update suggestion again for the current upstream release.
mods.snoozeDesc: Skip this update until a newer release appears upstream (currently v{{version}}). Use this when the website's announced version doesn't match the file's actual version.

toast.pinned: Frozen {{name}} — updates and profile changes will skip it
toast.repairNoGithub: '{{name}}' has no GitHub source linked — repair fetches from GitHub. Add a source via Edit sources… first.
toast.snoozed: Skipped updates for {{name}} until next release
toast.noGitHubInNexus: No GitHub link found in Nexus description for {{name}}
toast.foundNexusMod: Found Nexus mod: {{name}}. Open it on Nexus and click "Slow Download" / "Manual" — the app will catch the zip from your Downloads folder.

mods.title: Your mods

sort.label: Sort
sort.visualOnly: Visual only: this sort does not change load order.

mods.rollbackNoSource: '{{name}}' has no GitHub source linked — rollback fetches from GitHub. Add a source via Edit sources… first.
mods.rollbackConfirmBody: This will delete the current on-disk install and recover a compatible GitHub release. If this install still has a usable version, rollback picks the closest lower release; if the version reads "unknown", it reinstalls the last known-good release. Use it when the newest release is broken. Make sure STS2 is closed.
mods.rollbackConfirmWarning: Rollback is a beta recovery feature. It preserves mod configs and resolves a compatible GitHub release before deleting files, but some mods package releases inconsistently, so check the result before launching a long run.
mods.rollbackDesc: Recover a compatible GitHub release. Uses the previous release when the current version is known, or the last known-good release when this install reads "unknown".
mods.rollbackNeedSource: Link a GitHub source first via "Edit sources..." - rollback fetches from GitHub.

devBuilds.title: Dev Builds
devBuilds.subtitle: Switch the (Dev) app between open PRs' builds. Your release app is untouched.
devBuilds.empty: No open dev builds — open a PR and label it dev-build.
devBuilds.installing: Installing PR #{{pr}} — the (Dev) app will replace itself and relaunch.
devBuilds.noWindowsBuild: No Windows build (its build leg may have failed)
devBuilds.backToRelease: To return to the stable app, just launch your release STS2 Mod Manager — it's a separate, untouched install.
